best_edges.action: protect and use as baseline throughput engine
best_edges.evidence: pullback_long is the dominant edge: 32 trades, $2530.4 PnL, 259.1 PnL/exposure hour, 100% win rate, median hold 12.0m. It carries tail risk at -403.2 MAE, but broad pruning would risk the largest PnL source.

best_edges.action: protect fast winners, avoid blunt VWAP-extension caps
best_edges.evidence: charm_pin_pullback_long produced 34 trades and $1325.1 with median hold 3.8m and 200.4 PnL/exposure hour. Its average MAE is modest versus its worst tail, so the edge is real but path-dependent.

best_edges.action: protect as 1DTE fallback; do not directly tighten yet
best_edges.evidence: breakout_long_1dte contributed $1310.2 over 20 VALID_1DTE_ONLY trades. Prior direct 1DTE quality tightening repeatedly reduced net profit and score, so the existing fallback should be preserved.
squeeze_long produced $721 over 10 trades with worst MAE -208.25 and median MFE 77.8. It has cleaner tail behavior than pullback/charm-pin and may support profit expansion if convexity is preserved.

charm_wall_breakaway_long still contributes $532.6 from only 3 trades with median MFE 146.2, but latest admission/VWAP cap variant hurt score by -203 and net profit by -204.4. The edge is convex but fragile.

call_wall_acceptance_long had 1 trade, $91 PnL, 455 PnL/exposure hour, worst MAE -11.7, and high setup quality. Evidence is too small for promotion, but quality is strong enough for isolated sample-building.

broken_edges.action: freeze direct quality tightening; only test companion split
broken_edges.evidence: Direct 1DTE tightening experiments lowered score by -355 to -594 and net profit by -317 to -558 while worsening MAE. Current 1DTE baseline is profitable and should not be compressed.
broken_edges.problem: Direct breakout_long_1dte tightening has negative expectancy versus baseline.

broken_edges.action: do not repeat broad TP/VWAP expansion
broken_edges.evidence: A prior pullback TP/VWAP expansion increased drawdown to 2%, worsened worst MAE to -2026.5, and cut score by -1731 despite tiny net-profit improvement.
broken_edges.problem: Pullback expansion can monetize more exposure but creates unacceptable hidden tail risk.

broken_edges.action: avoid latest charm-wall VWAP cap geometry
broken_edges.evidence: The latest charm_wall_breakaway_long follow-through lost $204.4 and 203 score points despite no MAE improvement. Small sample and convex exits make cap-based trimming unreliable.
broken_edges.problem: Charm-wall admission cap/geometry changes are overfitting and reducing convex payoff.

broken_edges.action: do not optimize until activated with evidence
broken_edges.evidence: breakout_long, wall_reclaim_long, wall_breakout_retest_long, late_day_pin_fade, negative_gamma_momentum_long, and trend_day_pullback_long produced zero trades in this sample.
broken_edges.problem: Dormant or unreachable active-router profiles have no realized edge in the current evidence.

broken_edges.evidence: Hidden MAE signatures often retain only 75-85% of PnL and still leave worst MAE at -513.4 or -403.2. They remove too much good flow for a profit-expansion track.
broken_edges.problem: Single-feature MAE filters are too destructive for risk-adjusted profit expansion.

market_regime_read: Short, high-win-rate, take-profit-only sample over about 12.3 calendar days. Realized losses and slow >=12h trades are absent, but hidden MAE is still the binding risk: average MAE about -101, worst -513.4, and only 9.4% of trades print MFE before MAE. The portfolio edge is real but fragile: most PnL comes from pullback_long, charm_pin_pullback_long, breakout_long_1dte, and squeeze_long. Profit expansion should focus on preserving these engines while adding narrowly attributed convex or companion splits, not broad entry pruning.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Separates 1DTE pullback/reclaim states from breakout continuation without damaging the profitable breakout fallback.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Router overlap may cannibalize breakout_long_1dte, or the companion profile may be unreachable.
new_strategy_ideas.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Split
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VALID_1DTE_ONLY trades with ES reclaim/pullback, setup quality, VWAP distance, and baseline breakout_long_1dte attribution.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: 0DTE unavailable, 1DTE map valid, and ES state resembles pullback/reclaim rather than clean breakout continuation.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL >= $1310, 1DTE average MAE improves versus -123.9, total net profit >=98% of leader, and breakout_long_1dte retained PnL is explicitly measured.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Catches delayed high-extension charm-pin trades that static entry filters miss while preserving fast extended-VWAP winners.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: May overfit a small number of delayed recoveries or block profitable high-extension winners.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Charm Pin Early-Progress Tail Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Intratrade path features: first +1.5/+2.0 point timing, MFE-before-MAE, first 15m MFE/MAE, VWAP extension, rolling-high distance, and protected fast-winner UTCS.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: charm_pin_pullback_long high-extension or recent-high-rejection entry that fails to show early positive progress.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Reduce charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE toward <= -403 while retaining >=90% charm-pin PnL, >=95% protected fast extended-VWAP winners, total net profit >=98% of leader, and slow_12h remains 0.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Uses existing dormant playbook logic to split pin-avoid states away from charm-pin/charm-wall profiles.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Dormant profile may add no trades, over-route existing winners, or dilute protected fast charm-pin flow.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Charm Wall Pin-Avoid Dormant Activation Probe
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Router attribution, activated profile standalone PnL, cannibalization of charm_pin_pullback_long and charm_wall_breakaway_long, and protected fast-winner retention.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE charm/pin context where current charm_pin_pullback_long or charm_wall_breakaway_long entries show pin-avoid or rejection risk.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Activated profile has positive standalone PnL or reduces combined charm/pin worst MAE while total net profit remains >=98% of leader and protected fast-winner retention stays >=95%.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Adds high-quality call-wall acceptance samples without loosening global option-flow gates.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: One-trade evidence may be noise; looser gates can admit weak call-wall chases.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Call Wall Acceptance Isolated Sample Builder
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: call_wall_acceptance_long candidate rows, early-strike acceptance fields, call-volume increase, forward DEX, net DEX volume, MAE, MFE, and exposure.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE call-wall acceptance with high setup quality, strong ADX, and controlled MAE similar to the observed winner.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: At least 3 total call_wall_acceptance_long trades, positive standalone PnL, PnL/exposure >=250, worst MAE <= -150, and total net profit >=98% of leader.

next_experiments.config_bias: Keep current pullback_long admission mostly stable. Test only small realization-efficiency or path-state segmentation ideas that preserve at least 90-95% of pullback PnL; avoid broad VWAP-distance expansion or TP expansion.
next_experiments.goal: Improve portfolio PnL per exposure hour without damaging the largest PnL engine.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The pullback_long edge is strong but some longer, lower-quality recoveries tie capital; a narrow efficiency split can improve exposure-adjusted return while keeping core winners.
next_experiments.name: Pullback Core Efficiency Holdout
next_experiments.primary_metric: Total risk-adjusted score and pullback_long PnL/exposure, with pullback PnL retention >=95% and worst MAE no worse than -403.2.
next_experiments.risk: Any blunt filter can remove the dominant $2530 PnL source and lower total score despite prettier MAE.

next_experiments.config_bias: Prefer restoring or preserving squeeze convexity rather than compressing targets. Keep option-map and order-book confirmation strong; only explore small admission expansion where squeeze worst MAE remains bounded.
next_experiments.goal: Increase squeeze_long PnL contribution while preserving its relatively clean MAE profile.
next_experiments.hypothesis: squeeze_long has lower worst MAE than the main engines and may support more profit if continuation quality remains high.
next_experiments.name: Squeeze Convexity Preservation Probe
next_experiments.primary_metric: squeeze_long PnL, squeeze PnL/exposure, total score, and squeeze worst MAE <= -225.
next_experiments.risk: Small sample; adding weaker squeeze entries can dilute the clean tail profile or cannibalize pullback winners.

next_experiments.config_bias: Do not repeat latest charm-wall VWAP cap. Test only one small supported-admission or sample-builder variant at a time, preserving require-breakout and the wide convex payoff style.
next_experiments.goal: Recover charm-wall convex contribution without the latest score degradation.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The charm-wall edge pays through rare high-MFE moves; admission tuning must add or preserve high-convexity entries rather than shorten or cap them.
next_experiments.name: Charm Wall Convexity Recovery
next_experiments.primary_metric: charm_wall_breakaway_long PnL, profile PnL/exposure >=225, trade count, total score, and worst MAE no worse than -513.4.
next_experiments.risk: Very small sample; admission changes can overfit and reduce total PnL more than they improve exposure.

next_experiments.config_bias: Leave breakout_long_1dte baseline thresholds stable. If legal, add pullback_long_1dte through the one-dte router as an attribution experiment and measure cannibalization explicitly.
next_experiments.goal: Expand 1DTE profit without repeating failed direct 1DTE tightening.
next_experiments.hypothesis: VALID_1DTE_ONLY flow may contain both breakout and pullback/reclaim states; a companion split can improve MAE or PnL without harming the profitable breakout fallback.
next_experiments.name: 1DTE Companion Router Split
next_experiments.primary_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL, 1DTE average/worst MAE, total score, and breakout_long_1dte retained PnL.
next_experiments.risk: Router ordering can steal profitable breakout_long_1dte trades or produce no reachable companion trades.

next_experiments.config_bias: Use a full valid-0DTE router replacement only if activating one dormant pin-avoid profile. Keep tuning minimal and treat the result as attribution, not promotion.
next_experiments.goal: Discover whether existing dormant pin-avoid logic can split tail-heavy charm/pin states.
next_experiments.hypothesis: Some charm_pin_pullback_long and charm_wall_breakaway_long MAE may belong to a distinct pin-avoid regime already modeled in the playbook.
next_experiments.name: Dormant Pin-Avoid Attribution Probe
next_experiments.primary_metric: Activated profile standalone PnL, combined charm-pin/charm-wall worst MAE, total net profit retention >=98%, and protected fast-winner retention >=95%.
next_experiments.risk: Dormant activation can cannibalize known profitable charm-pin flow or be unreachable in the current sample.

next_experiments.config_bias: Nudge only the already-enabled extended-VWAP rejection near-miss family or equivalent profile-aware boundaries. Avoid global setup floors, broad structure floors, and hard VWAP caps because protected extended-VWAP winners are valuable.
next_experiments.goal: Reduce hidden MAE without sacrificing the fast extended-VWAP profit engine.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The worst charm-pin tail missed an enabled extended-VWAP veto by a narrow boundary, but protected extended-VWAP winners require surgical treatment.
next_experiments.name: Extended-VWAP Near-Miss Boundary Study
next_experiments.primary_metric: Worst MAE, retained total net profit >=98%, retained protected extended-VWAP winners >=95%, and PnL/exposure flat or better.
next_experiments.risk: Thresholds that catch the tail may also remove clean extended-VWAP winners and reduce profit more than risk.

research_notes: Use run_029/run_037/run_038 as the true leader baseline, not run_039, because run_039 is lower score and lower net profit after the latest charm-wall change. The next profit-expansion plan should avoid repeating failed families: direct 1DTE tightening, broad pullback TP/VWAP expansion, charm-wall VWAP cap compression, and broad hidden-signature pruning. The most useful research axis is additive or split-based: preserve pullback/charm-pin/1DTE engines, sample-build squeeze/call-wall/charm-wall convexity, and use dormant pin-avoid only as attribution.
risk_controls: Require total net profit >=98% of leader for any profit-expansion candidate.
Do not accept experiments that improve PnL/exposure only by starving completed trades more than 10% unless worst MAE improves materially and total score improves.
Track protected fast-winner and extended-VWAP-winner retention explicitly; target >=95% retention for any entry-filter experiment.
Reject broad filters that reduce PnL below 90% of leader even if they improve worst MAE.
Measure profile cannibalization for every router or dormant activation experiment.
